//NodeRegistry: TimeoutSeconds — how long a node can miss heartbeats before it's evicted. ReaperIntervalSeconds — how often the reaper sweeps.
//Dispatcher: TimeoutSeconds — max wall-clock time a single inference job may take before the coordinator gives up on it.
//Router: AffinitySlidingMinutes — sticky-conversation idle expiry. AffinityLoadBreakThreshold — how many extra in-flight jobs the sticky node may have before we break affinity. Strategy (v2.8) — how capable nodes are ranked: 'least-busy' (default, fewest in-flight jobs — the pre-v2.8 behaviour, bit-for-bit) or 'throughput' (measured tokens/sec, EWMA, adjusted for in-flight load; a node with no measurement is treated as average, never as slow). Sticky affinity still wins where it applies. Opt-in for one release.

Auth.//ApiKeys: Set real values via env (Auth__ApiKeys__0=...) or user-secrets. Empty list = no remote access. Entries here are anonymous clients with no limits — for named clients with quotas, use Clients below.
Auth.//Clients: Named clients (phase 25): an id, a key, and optional limits. All limits are nullable; null = unlimited. AllowedModels empty = all models; a model outside the list is a 404 identical to a model that does not exist. Over a rate limit = 429 + Retry-After; over TokensPerDay = 402. Keys via env (Auth__Clients__0__Key=...) or user-secrets, never here. The flat ApiKeys list above keeps working unchanged.
Auth.//AdminApiKeys: Bearer tokens that unlock /api/admin (cordon, uncordon, deregister). Separate from ApiKeys. Empty list = no remote admin access (loopback still honours RequireAuthForLoopback).
Auth.//RequireAuthForLoopback: If true, even loopback requests must present a Bearer token. Applies to both ApiKeys and AdminApiKeys.
Auth.//NodeEnrollmentSecret: Set via env (Auth__NodeEnrollmentSecret) or user-secrets. Empty = nodes cannot connect.

//VectorStore: Optional persistent vector store. Disabled by default. Provider selects the backend: 'local' (file-backed, replicated to the GPU fleet, self-healing) or 'postgres' (external PostgreSQL + pgvector — HNSW search, transactions, backups; no node replication). Distance/DefaultEmbeddingModel/Retrieval apply to both.

//VectorStore.example.//Postgres: Used only when Provider=postgres. ConnectionString is REQUIRED — set it via env (VectorStore__Postgres__ConnectionString) or user-secrets, never here. Schema/TablePrefix become SQL identifiers (^[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*$). Index: hnsw | ivfflat | none. Note: pgvector's ANN index tops out at 2000 dimensions.
//VectorStore.example.//Retrieval: How RAG retrieves (phase 24). Mode: 'vector' (default) | 'keyword' (BM25) | 'hybrid' (both, fused by Reciprocal Rank Fusion). Per-request override via header X-InferHub-Retrieve-Mode; an unknown value is a 400, not a silent fallback. Defaults stay 'vector' so an existing deployment sees byte-identical results. Rerank: 'none' (default) | 'llm' — an opt-in pass that scores the top candidates with a fleet chat model (RerankModel, defaulting to the request's own model) and reorders them; per-request via X-InferHub-Rerank: true. It costs a round trip, hard-capped by RerankCandidates and RerankTimeoutSeconds, after which the un-reranked order is kept.

//Ingestion: Document ingestion (phase 23). Active only when VectorStore:Enabled — ingestion writes to the vector store and nowhere else. Upload text/Markdown/HTML/JSON/PDF to POST /api/collections/{name}/documents; the coordinator extracts, chunks, embeds the chunks on the GPU fleet, and stores them. There is NO OCR and there never will be: a PDF with no usable text layer is rejected with an error, because a bad extraction does not fail loudly — it succeeds quietly and fills the corpus with gibberish that retrieves confidently. The original file is never kept; chunks, a content hash and metadata are. EmbeddingModel empty = VectorStore:DefaultEmbeddingModel. Raising MaxDocumentBytes above ~30 MB also needs Kestrel's MaxRequestBodySize raised.
//Fallback: Cloud burst. When the fleet cannot serve a model, forward to an OpenAI-compatible upstream instead of returning 404. OFF BY DEFAULT, and deliberately so: sending a user's prompt to a third party because their GPU box was asleep is a betrayal if it happens by surprise. Only models named in ModelMap are ever eligible — the map is the consent. Every fallback response carries 'X-InferHub-Served-By: fallback', the status page counts them, and nothing is stored. Trigger: 'no-node' (default) or 'no-node-or-saturated'. ApiKey via env (Fallback__ApiKey) or user-secrets, never here.
//Usage: Usage accounting persistence (phase 25). 'none' (default) keeps counters in memory — a restart resets them, like everything else on the coordinator. 'postgres' writes each usage record to an append-only table over its OWN connection string (deliberately not coupled to VectorStore:Postgres — you may want one without the other). A usage record is a client id, a model, a kind, two token counts, a fallback flag and a timestamp. Never the prompt, never the completion, never a hash of either — and there is no flag to change that. ConnectionString via env (Usage__Postgres__ConnectionString) or user-secrets.
//Queue: Bounded queueing when every node holding a model is at its DECLARED MaxConcurrency (phase 25). A request waits up to MaxWaitSeconds for a slot, no more than MaxDepth at a time; past either bound it is a 503 with Retry-After. Nodes that declared no cap never queue. With Fallback Trigger=no-node-or-saturated, saturation bursts to the upstream INSTEAD of queueing.
//Metrics: Prometheus scrape endpoint (phase 28). GET /metrics serves the text exposition format; it measures nothing new — every number was already on /api/status, and this gives them a history and an alert. OpenScrape=false (default) puts /metrics behind Auth:AdminApiKeys: it is operational like /health, but unlike /health it exposes node names, model names, client ids and the shape of your traffic, and a scraper is not an inference client. Set true only on a trusted network; it does not open /api/admin/*.
